tEn: Absolute Zero: Reinforced Self-play Reasoning with Zero Data
tZh: 绝对零度：零数据下的自我对弈推理
sEn: Absolute Zero proposes a new RLVR paradigm where a model self-generates tasks and learns without any external data, achieving SOTA in coding and math reasoning.
sZh: 绝对零度提出了一种新的RLVR范式，模型无需任何外部数据，通过自我生成任务并学习，在编码和数学推理上达到SOTA。
absEn: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has shown promise in enhancing the reasoning capabilities of large language models by learning directly from outcome-based rewards. Recent RLVR works that operate under the zero setting avoid supervision in labeling the reasoning process, but still depend on manually curated collections of questions and answers for training. The scarcity of high-quality, human-produced examples raises concerns about the long-term scalability of relying on human supervision, a challenge already evident in the domain of language model pretraining. Furthermore, in a hypothetical future where AI surpasses human intelligence, tasks provided by humans may offer limited learning potential for a superintelligent system. To address these concerns, we propose a new RLVR paradigm called Absolute Zero, in which a single model learns to propose tasks that maximize its own learning progress and improves reasoning by solving them, without relying on any external data. Under this paradigm, we introduce the Absolute Zero Reasoner (AZR), a system that self-evolves its training curriculum and reasoning ability by using a code executor to both validate proposed code reasoning tasks and verify answers, serving as an unified source of verifiable reward to guide open-ended yet grounded learning. Despite being trained entirely without external data, AZR achieves overall SOTA performance on coding and mathematical reasoning tasks, outperforming existing zero-setting models that rely on tens of thousands of in-domain human-curated examples. Furthermore, we demonstrate that AZR can be effectively applied across different model scales and is compatible with various model classes.
absZh: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）通过直接从基于结果的奖励中学习，在增强大语言模型的推理能力方面显示出潜力。最近在零设置下工作的RLVR方法避免了标注推理过程，但仍依赖人工策划的问题和答案集进行训练。高质量人工样本的稀缺引发了对依赖人类监督的长期可扩展性的担忧，这在语言模型预训练领域已经显现。此外，在AI超越人类智能的假设未来中，人类提供的任务可能对超级智能系统的学习潜力有限。为解决这些问题，我们提出了一种新的RLVR范式，称为绝对零度，其中单个模型学习提出能最大化自身学习进度的任务，并通过解决这些任务来提高推理能力，无需依赖任何外部数据。在此范式下，我们引入了绝对零度推理器（AZR），该系统通过使用代码执行器来验证提出的代码推理任务和答案，从而自我进化其训练课程和推理能力，作为指导开放但基于学习的可验证奖励的统一来源。尽管完全在无外部数据的情况下训练，AZR在编码和数学推理任务上实现了总体SOTA性能，优于依赖数万个领域内人工策划示例的现有零设置模型。此外，我们证明AZR可有效应用于不同模型规模，并与多种模型类别兼容。

contrib.en: Proposes Absolute Zero paradigm eliminating all external data for reasoning model training.
contrib.zh: 提出绝对零范式，完全消除推理模型训练中的外部数据依赖。

contrib.en: Introduces Absolute Zero Reasoner (AZR) using self-play with code executor for task proposal and verification.
contrib.zh: 引入绝对零推理器（AZR），通过自博弈和代码执行器实现任务提出与验证。

contrib.en: Achieves SOTA on coding and math reasoning without any human-curated data.
contrib.zh: 在无任何人工标注数据的情况下，在编程和数学推理上达到最优性能。

contrib.en: Demonstrates strong cross-domain transfer: code training improves math reasoning significantly.
contrib.zh: 展示强跨域迁移能力：代码训练显著提升数学推理性能。

contrib.en: Shows scaling benefits: larger models yield greater performance gains under AZR.
contrib.zh: 展示规模扩展优势：更大模型在AZR下获得更大性能提升。

contrib.en: Identifies emergent cognitive behaviors like intermediate planning and task-specific token length growth.
contrib.zh: 识别出涌现的认知行为，如中间规划和任务特定的词元长度增长。

limits.en: Safety concerns observed in Llama-3.1-8B, termed 'uh-oh moment', requiring future oversight.
limits.zh: 在Llama-3.1-8B中观察到安全问题，称为“呃哦时刻”，需要未来监督。

limits.en: Limited to deterministic programs; stochastic programs not yet supported.
limits.zh: 仅限于确定性程序，尚不支持随机程序。

limits.en: Proposer training shows moderate benefit; further investigation needed for optimal task generation.
limits.zh: 提出者训练收益中等，最优任务生成需进一步研究。

limits.en: Evaluation only on coding and math; generalization to other domains like embodied AI unexplored.
limits.zh: 评估仅限于编程和数学，未探索到具身AI等其他领域的泛化。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Absolute Zero Reasoner (AZR) achieves state-of-the-art performance with ZERO DATA. Without relying on any gold labels or human-defined queries, Absolute Zero Reasoner trained using our proposed self-play approach demonstrates impressive general reasoning capabilities improvements in both math and coding, despite operating entirely out-of-distribution. Remarkably, AZR surpasses models trained on tens of thousands of expert-labeled in-domain examples in the combined average score across both domains.
items.capZh: 图 1：绝对零推理器（AZR）在零数据下实现了最先进的性能。在不依赖任何黄金标签或人类定义的问题的情况下，使用我们提出的自对弈方法训练的绝对零推理器在数学和编程方面都展示了令人印象深刻的通用推理能力提升，尽管完全在分布外运行。值得注意的是，AZR 在两个领域的综合平均得分上超过了在数万个专家标记的领域内示例上训练的模型。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Absolute Zero Paradigm. Supervised learning relies on human-curated reasoning traces for behavior cloning. Reinforcement learning from verified rewards, enables agents to self-learn reasoning, but still depends on expert-defined learning distribution and a respective set of curated QA pairs, demanding domain expertise and manual effort. In contrast, we introduce a new paradigm, Absolute Zero, for training reasoning models without any human-curated data. We envision that the agent should autonomously propose tasks optimized for learnability and learn how to solve them using an unified model. The agent learns by interacting with an environment that provides verifiable feedback, enabling reliable and continuous self-improvement entirely without human intervention.
items.capZh: 图 2：绝对零范式。监督学习依赖于人工策划的推理轨迹进行行为克隆。基于验证奖励的强化学习使智能体能够自我学习推理，但仍依赖于专家定义的学习分布和相应的一组策划的问答对，需要领域专业知识和人工努力。相比之下，我们引入了一种新的范式——绝对零，用于训练推理模型而无需任何人工策划的数据。我们设想智能体应自主提出针对可学习性优化的任务，并使用统一模型学习如何解决它们。智能体通过与提供可验证反馈的环境交互来学习，从而实现完全无需人工干预的可靠且持续的自我改进。

items.en: Large language models (LLMs) have recently achieved remarkable improvements in reasoning capabilities by employing Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) (Lambert et al., 2024). Unlike methods that explicitly imitate intermediate reasoning steps, RLVR uses only outcome-based feedback, enabling large-scale reinforcement learning over vast task datasets (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025; Team et al., 2025; Jaech et al., 2024; OpenAI, 2025b; a). A particularly compelling variant is the “zero” RLVR paradigm (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025), which forgoes any cold-start distillation data, using neither human-generated nor AI-generated reasoning traces, and applies RLVR directly on the base model with task rewards. However, these methods still depend heavily on expertly curated distributions of reasoning question–answer pairs, which raises serious concerns about their long-term scalability (Villalobos et al., 2024). As reasoning models continue to advance, the effort required to construct large-scale, high-quality datasets may soon become unsustainable (Yue et al., 2025). A similar scalability bottleneck has already been identified in the domain of LLM pretraining (Sutskever et al., 2024). Furthermore, as AI systems continue to evolve and potentially exceed human intellect, an exclusive dependence on human-designed tasks risks imposing constraints on their capacity for autonomous learning and growth (Hughes et al., 2024). This underscores the need for a new paradigm that begins to explore possibilities beyond the constraints of human-designed tasks and prepares for a future in which AI systems may surpass human intelligence.
items.zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）最近通过使用带可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）（Lambert 等人，2024）在推理能力方面取得了显著改进。与明确模仿中间推理步骤的方法不同，RLVR 仅使用基于结果的反馈，从而能够在大量任务数据集上进行大规模强化学习（DeepSeek-AI 等人，2025；Team 等人，2025；Jaech 等人，2024；OpenAI，2025b；a）。一个特别引人注目的变体是“零”RLVR 范式（DeepSeek-AI 等人，2025），它放弃了任何冷启动蒸馏数据，既不使用人类生成的也不使用 AI 生成的推理轨迹，并直接在基础模型上应用 RLVR 和任务奖励。然而，这些方法仍然严重依赖于专家策划的推理问答对分布，这引发了对其长期可扩展性的严重担忧（Villalobos 等人，2024）。随着推理模型的不断进步，构建大规模、高质量数据集所需的努力可能很快变得不可持续（Yue 等人，2025）。在 LLM 预训练领域已经发现了类似的可扩展性瓶颈（Sutskever 等人，2024）。此外，随着 AI 系统不断进化并可能超越人类智力，对人类设计任务的独家依赖可能会限制其自主学习和成长的能力（Hughes 等人，2024）。这凸显了需要一种新的范式，开始探索超越人类设计任务限制的可能性，并为 AI 系统可能超越人类智能的未来做好准备。

items.en: To this end, we propose “Absolute Zero”, a new paradigm for reasoning models in which the model simultaneously learns to define tasks that maximize learnability and to solve them effectively, enabling self-evolution through self-play without relying on external data. In contrast to prior self-play methods that are limited to narrow domains, fixed functionalities, or learned reward models that are prone to hacking (Silver et al., 2017; Chen et al., 2025; 2024), the Absolute Zero paradigm is designed to operate in open-ended settings while remaining grounded in a real environment. It relies on feedback from the environment as a verifiable source of reward, mirroring how humans learn and reason through interaction with the world, and helps prevent issues such as hacking with neural reward models (Hughes et al., 2024). Similar to AlphaZero (Silver et al., 2017), which improves through self-play, our proposed paradigm requires no human supervision and learns entirely through self-interaction. We believe the Absolute Zero paradigm represents a promising step toward enabling large language models to autonomously achieve superhuman reasoning capabilities.
items.zh: 为此，我们提出了“绝对零”，一种用于推理模型的新范式，其中模型同时学习定义最大化可学习性的任务并有效解决它们，从而通过自对弈实现自我进化，而无需依赖外部数据。与先前局限于狭窄领域、固定功能或容易受到攻击的学习奖励模型的自对弈方法（Silver 等人，2017；Chen 等人，2025；2024）相比，绝对零范式旨在开放环境中运行，同时保持基于真实环境。它依赖于来自环境的反馈作为可验证的奖励来源，模仿人类通过与世界互动来学习和推理的方式，并有助于防止诸如神经奖励模型被攻击等问题（Hughes 等人，2024）。类似于通过自对弈改进的 AlphaZero（Silver 等人，2017），我们提出的范式不需要人类监督，完全通过自我交互学习。我们相信绝对零范式代表了向使大型语言模型自主实现超人类推理能力迈出的有希望的一步。

items.en: Building on this new reasoning paradigm, we introduce the Absolute Zero Reasoner (AZR), which proposes and solves coding tasks. We cast code executor as an open-ended yet grounded environment, sufficient to both validate task integrity and also provide verifiable feedback for stable training. We let AZR construct three types of coding tasks: infer and reason about one particular element in a program, input, output triplet, which corresponds to three complementary modes of reasoning: induction, abduction, and deduction. We train the entire system end-to-end with a newly proposed reinforcement learning advantage estimator tailored to the multitask nature of the proposed approach.
items.zh: 基于这一新的推理范式，我们引入了绝对零推理器（AZR），它提出并解决编程任务。我们将代码执行器视为一个开放但基于真实的环境，足以验证任务完整性并提供可验证的反馈以进行稳定训练。我们让 AZR 构建三种类型的编程任务：推断和推理程序、输入、输出三元组中的特定元素，这对应于三种互补的推理模式：归纳、溯因和演绎。我们使用新提出的强化学习优势估计器对整个系统进行端到端训练，该估计器针对所提出方法的多任务性质进行了定制。

items.en: Despite being trained entirely without any in-distribution data, AZR demonstrates remarkable capabilities across diverse reasoning tasks in math and coding. In mathematics, AZR achieves competitive performance compared to zero reasoner models explicitly fine-tuned with domain-specific supervision. In coding tasks, AZR establishes a new state-of-the-art performance, surpassing models specifically trained with code datasets using RLVR. Furthermore, AZR outperforms all previous models by an average of 1.8 absolute points compared to models trained in the “zero” setting using in-domain data. These surprising results highlight that general reasoning skills can emerge without human-curated domain targeted data, positioning Absolute Zero as an promising research direction and AZR as a first pivotal milestone. Besides the remarkable results AZR achieved with zero human data for reasoning, we also make very interesting findings summarized below:
items.zh: 尽管完全没有任何分布内数据训练，AZR 在数学和编程的各种推理任务中展示了卓越的能力。在数学方面，AZR 与使用领域特定监督明确微调的零推理器模型相比取得了有竞争力的性能。在编程任务中，AZR 建立了新的最先进性能，超过了使用代码数据集通过 RLVR 专门训练的模型。此外，与在“零”设置下使用领域内数据训练的模型相比，AZR 平均高出 1.8 个绝对点。这些令人惊讶的结果突显了通用推理技能可以在没有人工策划的领域目标数据的情况下出现，将绝对零定位为一个有前途的研究方向，并将 AZR 定位为第一个关键里程碑。除了 AZR 在零人类数据推理方面取得的显著成果外，我们还做出了以下非常有趣的发现：

items.en: Code priors amplify reasoning. The base Qwen-Coder-7b model started with math performance 3.6 points lower than Qwen-7b. But after AZR training for both models, the coder variant surpassed the base by 0.7 points, suggesting that strong coding capabilities may potentially amplify overall reasoning improvements after AZR training.
items.zh: 代码先验放大推理。基础 Qwen-Coder-7b 模型在数学性能上比 Qwen-7b 低 3.6 个点。但在对两个模型进行 AZR 训练后，编码器变体超过了基础模型 0.7 个点，这表明强大的编码能力可能潜在地放大 AZR 训练后的整体推理改进。

items.en: Cross domain transfer is more pronounced for AZR. After RLVR, expert code models raise math accuracy by only 0.65 points on average, whereas AZR-Base-7B and AZR-Coder-7B trained on self-proposed code reasoning tasks improve math average by 10.9 and 15.2, respectively, demonstrating much stronger generalized reasoning capability gains.
items.zh: 跨领域迁移对于 AZR 更为显著。在 RLVR 之后，专家代码模型将数学准确率平均仅提高 0.65 个点，而 AZR-Base-7B 和 AZR-Coder-7B 在自我提出的代码推理任务上训练后，数学平均分分别提高了 10.9 和 15.2 个点，展示了更强的泛化推理能力增益。

items.en: Bigger bases yield bigger gains. Performance improvements scale with model size: the 3B, 7B, and 14B coder models gain +5.7, +10.2, and +13.2 points respectively, suggesting continued scaling is advantageous for AZR.
items.zh: 更大的基础模型带来更大的收益。性能改进随模型大小而扩展：3B、7B 和 14B 编码器模型分别获得 +5.7、+10.2 和 +13.2 个点，表明持续缩放对 AZR 有利。

items.en: Comments as intermediate plans emerge naturally. When solving code induction tasks, AZR often interleaves step-by-step plans as comments and code (Figure˜19), resembling the ReAct prompting framework (Yao et al., 2023). Similar behavior has been observed in much larger formal-math models such as DeepSeek Prover v2 (671B) (Ren et al., 2025). We therefore believe that allowing the model to use intermediate scratch-pads when generating long-form answers may be beneficial in other domains as well.
items.zh: 作为中间计划的注释自然出现。在解决代码归纳任务时，AZR 经常将逐步计划作为注释和代码交错（图 19），类似于 ReAct 提示框架（Yao 等人，2023）。在更大的形式数学模型（如 DeepSeek Prover v2（671B））（Ren 等人，2025）中也观察到了类似的行为。因此，我们相信允许模型在生成长形式答案时使用中间草稿可能在其他领域也有益。

items.en: Cognitive Behaviors and Token length depends on reasoning mode. Distinct cognitive behaviors—such as step-by-step reasoning, enumeration, and trial-and-error all emerged through AZR training, but different behaviors are particularly evident across different types of tasks. Furthermore token counts grow over AZR training, but the magnitude of increase also differs by task types: abduction grows the most because the model performs trial-and-error until output matches, whereas deduction and induction grow modestly.
items.zh: 认知行为和令牌长度取决于推理模式。不同的认知行为——如逐步推理、枚举和试错——都通过 AZR 训练出现，但不同行为在不同类型的任务中尤为明显。此外，令牌计数在 AZR 训练过程中增长，但增长幅度也因任务类型而异：溯因增长最多，因为模型进行试错直到输出匹配，而演绎和归纳增长适中。

items.en: Safety alarms ringing. We observe AZR with Llama3.1-8b occasionally produces concerning chains of thought, we term the “uh-oh moment”, example shown in Figure˜32, highlighting the need for future work on safety-aware training (Zhang et al., 2025a).
items.zh: 安全警报响起。我们观察到使用 Llama3.1-8b 的 AZR 偶尔会产生令人担忧的思维链，我们称之为“啊哦时刻”，示例如图 32 所示，这凸显了未来对安全感知训练的需求（Zhang 等人，2025a）。

The model trains to imitate the reference responses to minimize the conditional negative log-likelihood (Ouyang et al., 2022):
items.zh: SFT 需要任务-推理过程-答案演示的数据集 𝒟={(x,c⋆,y⋆)}，其中 x 是查询，c⋆ 是黄金思维链（CoT），y⋆ 是黄金答案，全部由人类专家或更高级的 AI 模型提供。模型训练以模仿参考响应，最小化条件负对数似然（Ouyang 等人，2022）：

items.en: However, at the frontier level, there’s no stronger model to distill from, and expert human labeling doesn’t scale well.
items.zh: 然而，在前沿水平，没有更强的模型可以蒸馏，且专家人工标注无法很好地扩展。

items.en: To move beyond the limits of pure imitation, RLVR only requires a dataset of task and answer 𝒟={(x,y⋆)}\mathcal{D}=\{(x,y^{\star})\}, without labeled rationale. RLVR allows the model to generate its own CoT and calculate a verifiable reward with the golden answer r​(y,y⋆)r(y,y^{\star}). However, the learning task distribution 𝒟\mathcal{D}, with its set of queries and gold answers are still labeled by human experts. The trainable policy πθ\pi_{\theta} is optimized to maximize expected reward:
items.zh: 为了超越纯模仿的限制，RLVR 只需要任务和答案的数据集 𝒟={(x,y⋆)}，无需标注的推理过程。RLVR 允许模型生成自己的 CoT，并使用黄金答案计算可验证的奖励 r(y,y⋆)。然而，学习任务分布 𝒟 及其查询和黄金答案集仍然由人类专家标注。可训练策略 πθ 被优化以最大化期望奖励：

items.en: In summary, both SFT and RLVR still rely on human-curated datasets of either queries, demonstrations, or verifiers, which ultimately limit scalability. The Absolute Zero paradigm removes this dependency by allowing the model to generate, solve, and learn from its own interactions with the environment entirely through self-play.
items.zh: 总之，SFT 和 RLVR 仍然依赖于人工策划的数据集（无论是查询、演示还是验证器），这最终限制了可扩展性。Absolute Zero 范式通过允许模型完全通过自对弈生成、解决并从自身与环境的交互中学习，消除了这种依赖。

items.en: We propose the Absolute Zero paradigm, where during training, the model simultaneously proposes tasks, solves them, and learns from both stages. No external data is required and the model learns entirely through self-play and experience, aided by some environment. We illustrate this paradigm in Figure˜2, which contrasts Absolute Zero with supervised learning and RLVR, highlighting how our approach eliminates the need for any human-curated data by enabling self-improving task proposal and solution through self-play.
items.zh: 我们提出 Absolute Zero 范式，在训练过程中，模型同时提出任务、解决任务，并从这两个阶段中学习。不需要外部数据，模型完全通过自对弈和经验，借助某些环境进行学习。我们在图 2 中展示了这一范式，将 Absolute Zero 与监督学习和 RLVR 进行对比，突出了我们的方法如何通过自对弈实现自我改进的任务提出和解决，从而消除对任何人工策划数据的需求。

items.en: To make the Absolute Zero setting concrete, we now define how one model can act both as the proposer and solver role. To aid understanding, we include an illustration in Figure˜3. Let πθ\pi_{\theta} be our parameterized language model, it is used to play two roles, proposer πθpropose\pi_{\theta}^{\text{propose}} and solver πθsolve\pi_{\theta}^{\text{solve}} during training.
items.zh: 为了使 Absolute Zero 设置具体化，我们现在定义一个模型如何同时扮演提出者和解决者的角色。为帮助理解，我们在图 3 中给出了一个图示。令 πθ 为我们的参数化语言模型，在训练过程中它扮演两个角色：提出者 πθpropose 和解决者 πθsolve。

items.capEn: Figure 3: The Absolute Zero Loop. The Absolute Zero loop begins with the agent π\pi proposing task τ\tau, which is transformed by ff with the environment ee into a validated problem (x,y⋆)(x,y^{\star}), and also emits a reward rproposer^{\text{propose}} for learnability. Then, a standard RL step follows: the agent solves xx by producing yy, receiving reward rsolver^{\text{solve}} from ee by matching with y⋆y^{\star}. πpropose\pi^{\text{propose}} and πsolve\pi^{\text{solve}} are jointly trained and this process can be repeated indefinitely.
items.capZh: 图 3：Absolute Zero 循环。Absolute Zero 循环从智能体 π 提出任务 τ 开始，该任务由 f 与环境 e 一起转化为一个经过验证的问题 (x,y⋆)，并发出一个用于可学习性的奖励 rpropose。然后，一个标准的强化学习步骤开始：智能体通过生成 y 来解决 x，从 e 接收奖励 rsolve（通过与 y⋆ 匹配）。πpropose 和 πsolve 被联合训练，这个过程可以无限重复。

items.en: The proposer first samples a proposed task conditioned on variable zz: τ∼πθpropose(⋅|z)\tau\sim\pi_{\theta}^{\text{propose}}(\cdot|z), which will then be validated and used to construct a valid reasoning task together with the environment ee: (x,y⋆)∼fe(⋅|τ)(x,y^{\star})\sim f_{e}(\cdot|\tau), where xx is the task query and y⋆y^{\star} is the gold label. Then the solver produces an answer y∼πθsolve(⋅∣x)y\sim\pi_{\theta}^{\text{solve}}(\,\cdot\mid x). Each proposed task τ\tau is scored by a learnability reward repropose​(τ,πθ)r^{\text{propose}}_{e}(\tau,\pi_{\theta}), which captures the expected improvement in πθ\pi_{\theta} after training on the task query xx. Moreover, the same policy also receives a solution reward resolve​(y,y⋆)r^{\text{solve}}_{e}(y,y^{\star}) for its answer to the task query xx, with the environment again serving as the verifier. A nonnegative coefficient λ\lambda balances the trade-off between exploring new, learnable tasks and improving the model’s reasoning and problem-solving abilities. We formally define the absolute zero setting’s objective as follows:
items.zh: 提出者首先在条件变量 z 下采样一个提出的任务：τ∼πθpropose(·|z)，然后该任务将被验证并用于与环境 e 一起构建一个有效的推理任务：(x,y⋆)∼fe(·|τ)，其中 x 是任务查询，y⋆ 是黄金标签。然后解决者生成答案 y∼πθsolve(·∣x)。每个提出的任务 τ 由一个可学习性奖励 rproposee(τ,πθ) 评分，该奖励捕捉了在任务查询 x 上训练后 πθ 的预期改进。此外，同一策略还因其对任务查询 x 的答案获得一个解决方案奖励 resolvee(y,y⋆)，环境再次充当验证器。一个非负系数 λ 平衡了探索新的、可学习任务与改进模型推理和问题解决能力之间的权衡。我们正式定义 Absolute Zero 设置的目标如下：

items.en: Notice that we shift the burden of scaling data away from human experts and onto the proposer policy πθpropose\pi_{\theta}^{\text{propose}} and the environment ee. These two roles are both responsible for defining/evolving the learning task distribution, validating proposed tasks, and providing grounded feedback that supports stable and self-sustainable training. When proposing, zz acts as a conditional variable that seeds generation of tasks. Practically, zz can be instantiated by sampling a small subset of past (task, answer) pairs from a continually updated task memory, yet there is no specific implementation tied to the paradigm. To guide the proposing process, we use a learnability reward rpropose​(τ,πθ)r^{\text{propose}}(\tau,\pi_{\theta}), which measures how much the model is expected to improve by solving a proposed task τ\tau. Moreover, the solver reward rsolve​(y,y∗)r^{\text{solve}}(y,y^{*}) evaluates the correctness of the model’s output. Together, these two signals guide the model to propose tasks that are both challenging and learnable, while also enhancing its reasoning abilities, ultimately enabling continuous improvement through self-play.
items.zh: 注意，我们将扩展数据的负担从人类专家转移到提出者策略 πθpropose 和环境 e 上。这两个角色共同负责定义/演化学习任务分布、验证提出的任务，并提供支持稳定和自持续训练的基础反馈。在提出时，z 作为一个条件变量，为任务的生成提供种子。实际上，z 可以通过从持续更新的任务记忆中采样一小部分过去的（任务，答案）对来实例化，但该范式没有绑定特定的实现。为了指导提出过程，我们使用可学习性奖励 rpropose(τ,πθ)，该奖励衡量模型通过解决提出的任务 τ 预期能改进多少。此外，解决者奖励 rsolve(y,y∗) 评估模型输出的正确性。这两个信号共同引导模型提出既具有挑战性又可学习的任务，同时增强其推理能力，最终通过自对弈实现持续改进。

items.en: In this section, we present Absolute Zero Reasoner (AZR) as the first attempt to embrace the Absolute Zero Paradigm. In AZR, an unified LLM serves as both a proposer and a solver: it generates tasks to evolve its learning curriculum and attempts to solve them to improve its reasoning capabilities. The model is trained jointly with both roles, learning to create tasks that push the boundary of reasoning capacity while enhancing its ability to solve them effectively (Section˜3.1). Within this self-play training paradigm, the model learns from three distinct type of coding tasks, which corresponding to three fundamental modes of reasoning: abduction, deduction and induction (Section˜3.2). Using coding tasks is motivated by the Turing-completeness of programming languages (Stuart, 2015) and empirical evidence that code-based training improves reasoning (Aryabumi et al., 2024). We adopt code as an open-ended, expressive, and verifiable medium for enabling reliable task construction and verification (Section˜3.3). Finally, the model is updated using a newly proposed advantage estimator designed for multitask learning (Section˜3.3.5). We outline the overall algorithm in Algorithm˜1 and highlight an illustration of our Absolute Zero Reasoner approach in Figure˜4. To expedite future exploration in this area, we also present several attempts that did not yield fruitful results but still warrant discussion in Appendix˜D.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们提出 Absolute Zero Reasoner (AZR) 作为拥抱 Absolute Zero 范式的首次尝试。在 AZR 中，一个统一的 LLM 同时充当提出者和解决者：它生成任务以演化其学习课程，并尝试解决这些任务以提高其推理能力。该模型以两种角色联合训练，学习创建推动推理能力边界的任务，同时增强其有效解决这些任务的能力（第 3.1 节）。在这个自对弈训练范式中，模型从三种不同类型的编码任务中学习，这些任务对应于三种基本推理模式：溯因、演绎和归纳（第 3.2 节）。使用编码任务的动机是编程语言的图灵完备性（Stuart, 2015）以及基于代码的训练能改善推理的经验证据（Aryabumi et al., 2024）。我们采用代码作为一种开放、表达力强且可验证的媒介，以实现可靠的任务构建和验证（第 3.3 节）。最后，模型使用一种新提出的、专为多任务学习设计的优势估计器进行更新（第 3.3.5 节）。我们在算法 1 中概述了整体算法，并在图 4 中突出了我们的 Absolute Zero Reasoner 方法的图示。为了加速该领域的未来探索，我们还介绍了几次未产生丰硕成果但仍值得讨论的尝试，见附录 D。

full.sec: 3.1 Two Roles in One: Proposer and Solver
full.secZh: 3.1 双重角色：提议者与求解者

items.en: Large language models are naturally suited for implementing AZR in a multitask learning context (Radford et al., 2019), as both the formulation of reasoning tasks and their solutions occur within a unified language space. To this end, we propose rewarding a single model for both generating high learning potential tasks and solving them effectively, as specified by the Absolute Zero objective in Equation˜3. At each iteration of the online rollout, AZR proposes new reasoning tasks by conditioning on the task type (as defined in Section˜3.2) and KK past self-generated examples. The model is explicitly prompted to generate tasks that differ from these examples, promoting diversity and broader coverage of the task space. These task proposals are filtered and transformed into valid reasoning tasks that can be verified using the environment, outlined later in Section˜3.3. AZR then attempts to solve these newly proposed tasks, receiving grounded feedback for its model responses. Both task proposal and problem solving are trained using reinforcement learning. We now outline the rewards used for each role.
items.zh: 大型语言模型天然适合在多任务学习环境中实现 AZR（Radford et al., 2019），因为推理任务的制定及其解决方案都发生在统一的语言空间中。为此，我们提出奖励单个模型既生成高学习潜力的任务又有效解决它们，如公式 3 中的 Absolute Zero 目标所指定。在在线展开的每次迭代中，AZR 通过以任务类型（如第 3.2 节所定义）和 K 个过去自生成的示例为条件，提出新的推理任务。模型被明确提示生成与这些示例不同的任务，以促进多样性和更广泛的任务空间覆盖。这些任务提议被过滤并转化为有效的推理任务，可以使用环境进行验证，详见第 3.3 节。然后 AZR 尝试解决这些新提出的任务，为其模型响应接收基础反馈。任务提出和问题解决都使用强化学习进行训练。我们现在概述每个角色使用的奖励。

items.en: Prior work has shown that setting appropriate task difficulty is critical for promoting effective learning in reasoning systems (Zeng et al., 2025b). Motivated by this, we design a reward function for the proposer that encourages generation of tasks with meaningful learning potential—neither too easy nor unsolvable for the current solver. Concretely, we use the same language model in its solver role to estimate the learnability of a proposed task, a similar type of reward used in unsupervised environment design literature (Sukhbaatar et al., 2018). We perform nn Monte Carlo rollouts of the solver and compute the average success rate: r¯solve=1n​∑i=1Nrsolve(i)\bar{r}_{\text{solve}}=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{N}r_{\text{solve}}^{(i)}. The proposer’s reward is then defined as:
items.zh: 先前的工作表明，设置适当的任务难度对于促进推理系统中的有效学习至关重要（Zeng et al., 2025b）。受此启发，我们为提出者设计了一个奖励函数，鼓励生成具有有意义学习潜力的任务——对当前解决者来说既不太容易也不可解。具体来说，我们使用同一个语言模型在其解决者角色中估计提出任务的可学习性，这是一种在无监督环境设计文献中使用的类似奖励（Sukhbaatar et al., 2018）。我们执行 n 次蒙特卡洛展开，并计算平均成功率：r̄solve = (1/n) Σ_{i=1}^{N} rsolve^{(i)}。提出者的奖励定义为：

items.capEn: Figure 4: Absolute Zero Reasoner Training Overview. At every iteration, Absolute Zero Reasoner first PROPOSES a batch of tasks, conditioned on past self-generated triplets stored in a buffer and a particular task type: abduction, deduction, or induction (Section˜3.2). From these generated tasks, Python is used to filter and construct valid code-based reasoning questions. A learnability reward rproposer_{\text{propose}} is also calculated for each proposed task as defined in Equation˜4. The Absolute Zero Reasoner then SOLVES the batch of reasoning questions. Python is used again to verify the generated responses and compute the accuracy reward rsolver_{\text{solve}} as described in Equation˜5. Finally, the Absolute Zero Reasoner is jointly updated using both rproposer_{\text{propose}} and rsolver_{\text{solve}} across all three task types, using TRR++ (Section˜3.3.5).
items.capZh: 图 4：Absolute Zero Reasoner 训练概览。在每次迭代中，Absolute Zero Reasoner 首先提出一批任务，以存储在缓冲区中的过去自生成三元组和特定任务类型（溯因、演绎或归纳，第 3.2 节）为条件。从这些生成的任务中，使用 Python 过滤并构建有效的基于代码的推理问题。对于每个提出的任务，还根据公式 4 计算可学习性奖励 rpropose。然后 Absolute Zero Reasoner 解决这批推理问题。再次使用 Python 验证生成的响应，并根据公式 5 计算准确率奖励 rsolve。最后，Absolute Zero Reasoner 使用 rpropose 和 rsolve 在所有三种任务类型上联合更新，使用 TRR++（第 3.3.5 节）。

items.en: The intuition is that if a task is either trivial to solve (r¯solve=1\bar{r}_{\text{solve}}=1) or unsolvable (r¯solve=0\bar{r}_{\text{solve}}=0), the task provides little to no learning signal for the proposer. In contrast, tasks of moderate difficulty, where the solver occasionally succeeds are rewarded the most, as they offer the richest feedback and greatest potential for learning.
items.zh: 直觉是，如果一个任务要么容易解决（r̄solve=1）要么不可解（r̄solve=0），则该任务为提出者提供很少或没有学习信号。相反，中等难度的任务（解决者偶尔成功）获得最高奖励，因为它们提供最丰富的反馈和最大的学习潜力。

items.en: For the solver, we assign a simple binary reward based on the correctness of its final output,
items.zh: 对于解决者，我们根据其最终输出的正确性分配一个简单的二元奖励：

items.en: where y⋆y^{\star} is the ground-truth answer, and equality is evaluated based on value equality in Python.
items.zh: 其中 y⋆ 是真实答案，相等性基于 Python 中的值相等性进行评估。

items.en: With the primary rewards for the proposing and solving roles defined, we adopt the following composite reward structure, which integrates rproposer_{\text{propose}} and rsolver_{\text{solve}} with a format-aware penalty inspired by DeepSeek-AI et al. (2025):
items.zh: 在定义了提出和解决角色的主要奖励后，我们采用以下复合奖励结构，该结构将 rpropose 和 rsolve 与一个格式感知惩罚相结合，灵感来自 DeepSeek-AI 等人 (2025)：

items.en: where yπy_{\pi} is the response of the language model. The main format that the proposing and solving tasks need to follow is the DeepSeek R1 <think> and <answer> format, as shown in Figure˜33. Moreover, for the proposer, the reward criterion for format goes beyond simply following the XML structure. As detailed in Section˜3.3.3, only responses that produce valid triplets and pass the filtering stage are considered to be correctly formatted.
items.zh: 其中 yπ 是语言模型的响应。提出和解决任务需要遵循的主要格式是 DeepSeek R1 的 <think> 和 <answer> 格式，如图 33 所示。此外，对于提出者，格式的奖励标准不仅仅是遵循 XML 结构。如第 3.3.3 节所述，只有产生有效三元组并通过过滤阶段的响应才被视为格式正确。

full.sec: 3.2 Learning Different Modes of Reasoning: Deduction, Induction, and Abduction
full.secZh: 3.2 学习不同推理模式：演绎、归纳与溯因

items.en: AZR uses code executor as both a flexible interface and a verifiable environment. This setup enables automatic construction, execution, and validation of code reasoning tasks (Stuart, 2015; Aryabumi et al., 2024). Give program space 𝒫\mathscr{P}, input space ℐ\mathscr{I} and output space 𝒪\mathscr{O} of a coding language, we define an AZR reasoning task as a triplet (p,i,o)(p,i,o), where p∈𝒫p\in\mathscr{P} is a program, i∈ℐi\in\mathscr{I} is an input, and o∈𝒪o\in\mathscr{O} is the corresponding output produced by running program on input, o=p​(i)o=p(i). AZR learns by reasoning about different parts of this task triplet, using three distinct core reasoning modes, each of which focuses on inferring one part of the triplet given the others:
items.zh: AZR 使用代码执行器作为灵活的接口和可验证的环境。这种设置使得代码推理任务的自动构建、执行和验证成为可能（Stuart, 2015; Aryabumi et al., 2024）。给定编程语言的程序空间 𝒫、输入空间 ℐ 和输出空间 𝒪，我们将 AZR 推理任务定义为一个三元组 (p,i,o)，其中 p∈𝒫 是一个程序，i∈ℐ 是一个输入，o∈𝒪 是通过在输入上运行程序产生的相应输出，o=p(i)。AZR 通过推理该任务三元组的不同部分来学习，使用三种不同的核心推理模式，每种模式专注于在给定其他部分的情况下推断三元组的一个部分：

items.en: Deduction: predicting the output oo given a program pp and input ii, capturing step-by-step logical reasoning.
items.zh: 演绎：给定程序 p 和输入 i，预测输出 o，捕捉逐步的逻辑推理。

items.en: As a proposer, AZR is conditioned on the task type α=deduction\alpha=\text{deduction} and KK reference examples from the deduction buffer 𝒟deduction\mathcal{D}_{\text{deduction}} (all task buffers are outlined in Section˜3.3), and generates a pair (p,i)(p,i). The environment ee then executes p​(i)p(i) to compute oo, completing the triplet (p,i,o)(p,i,o), which is added to the buffer if non-error output was produced.
items.zh: 作为提出者，AZR 以任务类型 α=deduction 和来自演绎缓冲区 𝒟deduction 的 K 个参考示例（所有任务缓冲区在第 3.3 节中概述）为条件，并生成一对 (p,i)。然后环境 e 执行 p(i) 以计算 o，完成三元组 (p,i,o)，如果产生了非错误输出，则将其添加到缓冲区。

items.en: As a solver, the model receives (p,i)(p,i) and predicts the output oπo_{\pi}. The predicted output is verified using type-aware value equality in python to account for possible variations (such as set ordering or fractions).
items.zh: 作为解决者，模型接收 (p,i) 并预测输出 oπ。预测的输出使用 Python 中类型感知的值相等性进行验证，以考虑可能的变体（例如集合顺序或分数）。

items.en: Abduction: inferring a plausible input ii given the program pp and an output oo, resembling trial-and-error or online search.
items.zh: 溯因：给定程序 p 和输出 o，推断一个合理的输入 i，类似于试错或在线搜索。

items.en: As a proposer, the policy πpropose\pi^{\text{propose}}’s input and output is almost the same as the proposer for the deduction task, except that the task type α=abduction\alpha=\text{abduction} is changed as an input. The model generates a pair (p,i)(p,i) conditioned on α\alpha and reference examples. Then we executes p​(i)p(i) and get the triplet (p,i,o)(p,i,o).
items.zh: 作为提议者，策略 πpropose 的输入和输出与演绎任务的提议者几乎相同，只是任务类型 α=abduction 作为输入被改变。模型生成一个对 (p,i)，条件于 α 和参考示例。然后我们执行 p(i) 并得到三元组 (p,i,o)。

items.en: As a solver, the model receives (p,o)(p,o) and predicts iπi_{\pi}. The solution is verified by checking whether p​(iπ)=op(i_{\pi})=o. Since programs may not be bijective, we use output value equivalence rather than requiring exact input matches.
items.zh: 作为求解器，模型接收 (p,o) 并预测 iπ。通过检查 p(iπ)=o 来验证解。由于程序可能不是双射的，我们使用输出值等价性而非要求精确的输入匹配。

items.en: Induction: synthesizing a program pp from a set of in-out examples {(in,on)}\{(i^{n},o^{n})\}, requiring generalization from partial information.
items.zh: 归纳：从一组输入-输出示例 {(in,on)} 中综合程序 p，要求从部分信息中泛化。

items.en: As a proposer, AZR samples a valid program pp from 𝒟abduction∪𝒟deduction\mathcal{D}_{\text{abduction}}\cup\mathcal{D}_{\text{deduction}}, generates NN new inputs and a message mm, and uses the environment to compute corresponding outputs. This forms an extended task representation (p,{(in,on)},m)(p,\{(i^{n},o^{n})\},m), which is stored in the induction buffer 𝒟induction\mathcal{D}_{\text{induction}}. Since infinitely many functions can map the inputs to the outputs, making the induction task under-constrained, the message mm helps properly condition the problem for the solver.
items.zh: 作为提议者，AZR 从 𝒟abduction∪𝒟deduction 中采样一个有效程序 p，生成 N 个新输入和一条消息 m，并使用环境计算相应的输出。这形成了一个扩展的任务表示 (p,{(in,on)},m)，存储在归纳缓冲区 𝒟induction 中。由于无限多的函数可以将输入映射到输出，使得归纳任务欠约束，消息 m 有助于为求解器适当地条件化问题。

items.en: As a solver, the model is shown the first half of the input-output pairs and the message mm, and must synthesize a program pπp_{\pi} that correctly maps the remaining hidden inputs to their outputs. The use of held-out examples discourages overfitting through if-else logic and promotes generalized induction.
items.zh: 作为求解器，模型被展示一半的输入-输出对和消息 m，必须综合一个程序 pπ，该程序正确地将剩余的隐藏输入映射到其输出。使用留出示例通过 if-else 逻辑阻止过拟合，并促进泛化的归纳。

items.capEn: Figure 5: The Seed AZR Zero Triplet. The above identity function triplet was the only triplet provided to AZR to initiate its self-bootstrap propose-and-solve RLVR loop. We note that the base LLM is fully capable of initiating the AZR loop without any seed program; its inclusion illustrates our approach’s flexibility: we can optionally initialize seed programs with existing datasets of varying complexity, and we initialized ours with the simplest program.
items.capZh: 图 5：种子 AZR 零三元组。上述恒等函数三元组是提供给 AZR 以启动其自举提议-求解 RLVR 循环的唯一三元组。我们注意到基础 LLM 完全能够在没有任何种子程序的情况下启动 AZR 循环；包含它说明了我们方法的灵活性：我们可以选择用不同复杂度的现有数据集初始化种子程序，而我们用最简单的程序初始化了我们的种子。

items.en: Each reasoning task type leverages code as an expressive and verifiable medium, aligning with the Absolute Zero Paradigm’s goals of fully self-improving systems in open-ended domains (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025; Lambert et al., 2024). All prompts used by three different task types and two types of roles within a task type are shown in Figures˜36, 34, 35, 39, 37 and 38. Next, we outline exact details of our algorithm.
items.zh: 每种推理任务类型利用代码作为表达性和可验证的媒介，与绝对零范式的目标一致，即在开放领域实现完全自我改进的系统（DeepSeek-AI 等，2025；Lambert 等，2024）。三种不同任务类型和任务类型内两种角色使用的所有提示显示在图 36、34、35、39、37 和 38 中。接下来，我们概述算法的确切细节。

full.secZh: 3.3 绝对零度推理器学习算法

items.en: In this section, we will discuss details of our AZR self-play algorithm, including initialization of buffers 3.3.1, usage of thse buffers 3.3.2, construction of valid tasks 3.3.3, validating solutions 3.3.4, and finally advantage estimator calculation 3.3.5. We outline the overall recipe of the self-play procedure of AZR in Algorithm˜1.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们将讨论 AZR 自博弈算法的细节，包括缓冲区初始化 3.3.1、缓冲区使用 3.3.2、有效任务构建 3.3.3、验证解 3.3.4，以及最终的优势估计器计算 3.3.5。我们在算法 1 中概述了 AZR 自博弈过程的整体方案。

items.en: To initialize AZR self-play, we first generate a seed set of valid triplets using the base language model. Each prompt samples up to KK triplets from the current seed buffer 𝒟seed\mathcal{D}_{\text{seed}} as references. When 𝒟seed\mathcal{D}_{\text{seed}} is empty at time 0, we fall back to the zero triplet show in Figure˜5. During the seeding stage, we use the same proposer prompts detailed in Figures˜34, 35 and 36.
items.zh: 为了初始化 AZR 自博弈，我们首先使用基础语言模型生成一组种子有效三元组。每个提示从当前种子缓冲区 𝒟seed 中采样最多 K 个三元组作为参考。当 𝒟seed 在时间 0 为空时，我们回退到图 5 中显示的零三元组。在种子阶段，我们使用图 34、35 和 36 中详述的相同提议者提示。

items.en: First, for deduction and abduction tasks, the LLM is prompted to generate (p,i)(p,i) pairs, which are filtered, executed, and stored as valid triplets. We initialize 𝒟abduction0=𝒟deduction0=𝒟seed\mathcal{D}^{0}_{\text{abduction}}=\mathcal{D}^{0}_{\text{deduction}}=\mathcal{D}_{\text{seed}}, where |𝒟seed|=B×S|\mathcal{D}_{\text{seed}}|=B\times S, where BB is the batch size, and S=4S=4 is a factor we fix in all experiments. All seed triplet’s program are stripped of global variables and comments (Appendix˜D), but subsequent iterations of adding new triplets to the buffers are unaltered. No model updates occur during this phase. Similarly, to initialize the induction buffer, we sample programs from 𝒟seed\mathcal{D}_{\text{seed}}, generate matching input sets and messages, and collect valid examples until |𝒟induction0|=B×S|\mathcal{D}^{0}_{\text{induction}}|=B\times S.
items.zh: 首先，对于演绎和溯因任务，LLM 被提示生成 (p,i) 对，这些对被过滤、执行并存储为有效三元组。我们初始化 𝒟abduction0=𝒟deduction0=𝒟seed，其中 |𝒟seed|=B×S，B 是批次大小，S=4 是我们在所有实验中固定的因子。所有种子三元组的程序都去除了全局变量和注释（附录 D），但后续迭代向缓冲区添加新三元组时保持不变。此阶段不进行模型更新。类似地，为了初始化归纳缓冲区，我们从 𝒟seed 中采样程序，生成匹配的输入集和消息，并收集有效示例直到 |𝒟induction0|=B×S。

items.en: During the actual self-play stage of AZR, we use the task buffer in three ways. First, for the proposer of abduction and deduction tasks, we uniformly sample KK past triplets from the buffer, present them as in-context examples to the proposer and let it generate a new task. The design is to show it past examples, and prompt it to generate a different one to promote diversity (Zhao et al., 2025a). Second, we sample one triplet from the union of abduction and deduction buffers 𝒟abd​⋃𝒟ded\mathcal{D}_{\text{abd}}\bigcup\mathcal{D}_{\text{ded}}, and present the program pp from that triplet to the induction proposer to generate a set of NN matching inputs {in}\{i^{n}\} and a natural language message mm. Lastly, to maintain stable training, if a batch of solver problems contains fewer than BB valid proposed tasks (proposer not adhering to formatting), we fill the remainder by uniformly sampling from the corresponding task buffer of previously validated triplets.
items.zh: 在 AZR 的实际自博弈阶段，我们以三种方式使用任务缓冲区。首先，对于溯因和演绎任务的提议者，我们从缓冲区中均匀采样 K 个过去的三元组，作为上下文示例呈现给提议者，并让其生成一个新任务。设计目的是展示过去的示例，并提示其生成不同的示例以促进多样性（Zhao 等，2025a）。其次，我们从溯因和演绎缓冲区的并集 𝒟abd⋃𝒟ded 中采样一个三元组，并将该三元组中的程序 p 呈现给归纳提议者，以生成一组 N 个匹配输入 {in} 和一条自然语言消息 m。最后，为了保持训练稳定，如果一批求解器问题包含少于 B 个有效提议任务（提议者未遵守格式），我们通过从相应任务缓冲区中均匀采样先前验证的三元组来填充剩余部分。

items.en: The buffer grows for abduction and deduction tasks whenever π\pi propose a valid triplet (p,i,o)(p,i,o), regardless if it gets any task reward. Similarly, for induction tasks, all valid triplets (p,{in,on}),m(p,\{i^{n},o^{n}\}),m are added to the buffer.
items.zh: 对于溯因和演绎任务，每当 π 提议一个有效三元组 (p,i,o) 时，缓冲区就会增长，无论它是否获得任何任务奖励。类似地，对于归纳任务，所有有效三元组 (p,{in,on}),m 都被添加到缓冲区。

items.en: Proposal Task Validation. We first describe how we construct valid tasks from the proposals generated by the policy π\pi. For deduction and abduction tasks, each proposal consists of a program and an input (p,i)(p,i). To validate the task, we use the task validation procedure (steps shown below) on the input to obtain the correct output oo, resulting in a complete triplet (p,i,o)(p,i,o). For induction tasks, given a program pp the policy proposes a set of inputs {in}\{i^{n}\} and message mm. We also use the task validation procedure on each of the input ini^{n} in the set to obtain a corresponding output ono^{n}, forming a set of input-output pairs {in,on}\{i^{n},o^{n}\}. We do not impose any constraints on mm. The resulting task is considered valid only when all inputs yield valid outputs and the formatting requirements are satisfied. The task validation procedure entails:
items.zh: 提议任务验证。我们首先描述如何从策略 π 生成的提议中构建有效任务。对于演绎和溯因任务，每个提议由一个程序和一个输入 (p,i) 组成。为了验证任务，我们对输入使用任务验证程序（步骤如下）以获得正确的输出 o，从而得到一个完整的三元组 (p,i,o)。对于归纳任务，给定一个程序 p，策略提议一组输入 {in} 和消息 m。我们还对集合中的每个输入 in 使用任务验证程序以获得相应的输出 on，形成一组输入-输出对 {in,on}。我们对 m 不施加任何约束。只有当所有输入都产生有效输出且满足格式要求时，结果任务才被视为有效。任务验证程序包括：

items.en: Program Integrity. We first use Python to run the program pp with the input ii. If no errors are raised and something is returned, we then gather the output oo of that (p,i)(p,i) pair and determine that the program at least has valid syntax.
items.zh: 程序完整性。我们首先使用 Python 运行程序 p 和输入 i。如果没有引发错误并且返回了内容，则收集该 (p,i) 对的输出 o，并确定该程序至少具有有效语法。

items.en: Program Safety. We also check whether a program is safe for execution by restricting the use of certain sensitive packages that might cause harm to the Python environment, i.e., os.sys, sys, shutil. The list of packages used to filter out invalid programs is provided in Figure˜8. This list is also included in the instructions when prompting the language model to generate questions. See Figures˜34, 35 and 36.
items.zh: 程序安全性。我们还通过限制使用某些可能对 Python 环境造成危害的敏感包（例如 os.sys、sys、shutil）来检查程序是否安全执行。用于过滤无效程序的包列表在图 8 中提供。该列表也包含在提示语言模型生成问题时的指令中。见图 34、35 和 36。

items.en: Check for Determinism. In our setting, we only consider deterministic programs, i.e., p∈𝒫deterministic⊂𝒫p\in\mathscr{P}_{\text{deterministic}}\subset\mathscr{P}, where 𝒫\mathscr{P} is the space of all valid programs and ℐ\mathscr{I} is the space of all valid inputs:
items.zh: 确定性检查。在我们的设置中，我们只考虑确定性程序，即 p∈𝒫deterministic⊂𝒫，其中 𝒫 是所有有效程序的空间，ℐ 是所有有效输入的空间：

items.en: where (j)(j) indexes repeated independent executions of the program. That is, for all inputs ii, the output of p​(i)p(i) remains identical with any independent execution of the program. A valid program/input/output triplet (p,i,o)(p,i,o) is defined such that o=p​(i)o=p(i), where p∈𝒫deterministicp\in\mathscr{P}_{\text{deterministic}}.
items.zh: 其中 (j) 索引程序的重复独立执行。也就是说，对于所有输入 i，p(i) 的输出在程序的任何独立执行中保持不变。一个有效的程序/输入/输出三元组 (p,i,o) 定义为 o=p(i)，其中 p∈𝒫deterministic。

items.en: Since the output of probabilistic programs can vary on every individual run, it is non-trivial to use verifiable functions to evaluate the correctness of an answer. Therefore, to keep the verifier simple, we restrict the valid programs generated by the learner to the class of deterministic programs. We believe that stochastic programs can encompass a larger class of behaviors and are important and promising to include in future versions of AZR.
items.zh: 由于概率程序的输出在每次单独运行时可能不同，因此使用可验证函数来评估答案的正确性并非易事。因此，为了保持验证器简单，我们将学习者生成的有效程序限制为确定性程序类。我们相信随机程序可以包含更广泛的行为，并且对于未来版本的 AZR 来说是重要且有前景的。

items.en: To implement the filtering of invalid probabilistic programs, and following the definition of a deterministic program highlighted in Equation˜7, we approximate this procedure by independently running the program jj finite times and checking that all the outputs are equal. For computational budget reasons, we fixed j=2j=2 for all experiments.
items.zh: 为了实现无效概率程序的过滤，并遵循方程 7 中强调的确定性程序定义，我们通过独立运行程序 j 次有限次数并检查所有输出是否相等来近似此过程。出于计算预算原因，我们在所有实验中固定 j=2。

items.en: Solving Task Construction. If a task proposal passes these three checks, we deem it a valid task and apply appropriate procedures to present part of the triplet to the solver. Specifically, we set x=(p,i)x=(p,i) for deduction; x=(p,o)x=(p,o) for abduction; and x=({in,on}n=1N⁣/⁣/2,m)x=(\{i^{n},o^{n}\}^{N//2}_{n=1},m) for induction, where half of the tests cases and a program description mm is used. We use all valid tasks from timestep tt; if the batch BB is not full, we uniformly sample from previously validated tasks to fill the batch.
items.zh: 求解任务构建。如果任务提议通过了这三项检查，我们将其视为有效任务，并应用适当的程序将三元组的一部分呈现给求解器。具体来说，对于演绎，我们设置 x=(p,i)；对于溯因，x=(p,o)；对于归纳，x=({in,on}n=1N//2,m)，其中使用了一半的测试用例和程序描述 m。我们使用时间步 t 的所有有效任务；如果批次 B 未满，我们均匀地从先前验证的任务中采样以填充批次。

items.en: For abduction task, we receive iπi_{\pi} from the solver policy, then we equivalence match using p​(iπ)=p​(i⋆)p(i_{\pi})=p(i^{\star}), where ∗* refers to the privileged gold information. The reason we do not just match iπi_{\pi} and i⋆i^{\star} is because pp is not necessarily bijective. For deduction task, we match oπ=o⋆o_{\pi}=o^{\star}. For induction, we match all⁡({pπ​(in⋆)=on⋆}N)\operatorname{all}(\{p_{\pi}(i_{n}^{\star})=o_{n}^{\star}\}^{N}). This part might be convoluted to explain in language, therefore we recommend the reader to see how we did abduction, deduction and induction verification in code in Figures˜10, 11 and 12, respectively.
items.zh: 对于溯因任务，我们从求解器策略接收 iπi_{\pi}，然后通过 p​(iπ)=p​(i⋆)p(i_{\pi})=p(i^{\star}) 进行等价匹配，其中 ∗* 表示特权黄金信息。我们不直接匹配 iπi_{\pi} 和 i⋆i^{\star} 的原因是 pp 不一定是双射。对于演绎任务，我们匹配 oπ=o⋆o_{\pi}=o^{\star}。对于归纳，我们匹配 all⁡({pπ​(in⋆)=on⋆}N)\operatorname{all}(\{p_{\pi}(i_{n}^{\star})=o_{n}^{\star}\}^{N})。这部分用语言解释可能比较复杂，因此我们建议读者参考图 10、11 和 12 中的代码，了解我们如何分别进行溯因、演绎和归纳验证。

items.en: Since AZR trains the combination of roles and task types, it operates in a multitask reinforcement learning setup (Zhang & Yang, 2021; Zhao et al., 2022; Wang et al., 2023; Yue et al., 2023). Instead of computing a single global baseline as in REINFORCE++ (Hu, 2025) (Appendix˜A), we compute separate baselines for each of the six task-role configurations. This can be viewed as an interpolation between per-question baselines, as in GRPO (Shao et al., 2024), and a global baseline, allowing for more structured variance reduction tailored to each task setup. We refer to this variant as Task-Relative REINFORCE++ (TRR++). The normalized advantage AnormA^{\text{norm}} is computed as:
items.zh: 由于 AZR 训练角色和任务类型的组合，它运行在多任务强化学习设置中（Zhang & Yang, 2021; Zhao et al., 2022; Wang et al., 2023; Yue et al., 2023）。我们不计算像 REINFORCE++（Hu, 2025）（附录 A）中的单个全局基线，而是为六种任务-角色配置分别计算基线。这可以看作是每问题基线与全局基线之间的插值，类似于 GRPO（Shao et al., 2024），从而为每个任务设置提供更结构化的方差缩减。我们将此变体称为任务相对 REINFORCE++（TRR++）。归一化优势 AnormA^{\text{norm}} 计算如下：

items.en: where the mean and standard deviation are computed within each task type and role, yielding six baselines.
items.zh: 其中均值和标准差在每个任务类型和角色内计算，产生六个基线。

items.en: For all experiments, we initialize the buffers as described in Section˜3.1. AZR models are trained using a batch size of 64×664\times 6 (2 roles ×\times 3 task types). We use constant learning rate=1​e−6=1e{-6} and the AdamW optimizer (Loshchilov & Hutter, 2019). Complete list of hyperparameters is provided in Table˜3.
items.zh: 对于所有实验，我们按照第 3.1 节所述初始化缓冲区。AZR 模型使用批大小 64×664\times 6（2 个角色 ×\times 3 个任务类型）进行训练。我们使用恒定学习率 =1e−6=1e{-6} 和 AdamW 优化器（Loshchilov & Hutter, 2019）。超参数的完整列表见表 3。

items.en: For the main experiments, we train AZR models on Qwen2.5-7B and Qwen2.5-7B-Coder, resulting in Absolute Zero Reasoner-base-7B and Absolute Zero Reasoner-Coder-7B, respectively. Additional experiments include training Qwen2.5-Coder-3B, Qwen2.5-Coder-14B, Qwen2.5-14B, Llama-3.1-8B (Yang et al., 2024a; Hui et al., 2024; Dubey et al., 2024).
items.zh: 对于主要实验，我们在 Qwen2.5-7B 和 Qwen2.5-7B-Coder 上训练 AZR 模型，分别得到 Absolute Zero Reasoner-base-7B 和 Absolute Zero Reasoner-Coder-7B。额外实验包括训练 Qwen2.5-Coder-3B、Qwen2.5-Coder-14B、Qwen2.5-14B、Llama-3.1-8B（Yang et al., 2024a; Hui et al., 2024; Dubey et al., 2024）。

items.en: To evaluate our models, we divide the datasets into in-distribution (ID) and out-of-distribution (OOD) categories. For OOD benchmarks, which we emphasize more, we further categorize them into coding and mathematical reasoning benchmarks. For coding tasks, we evaluate using Evalplus (Liu et al., 2023) on the HumanEval+ and MBPP+ benchmarks, as well as LiveCodeBench Generation (v1-5, May 23-Feb 25) (Jain et al., 2024). For mathematical reasoning, we utilize six standard benchmarks commonly used in recent zero-shot trained reasoners: AIME’24, AIME’25, OlympiadBench (He et al., 2024), Minerva, Math500 (Hendrycks et al., 2021), and AMC’23. For ID benchmarks, we use CruxEval-I(nput), CruxEval-O(utput), and LiveCodeBench-Execution (Gu et al., 2024; Jain et al., 2024), which assess reasoning capabilities regarding the input and output of programs (Li et al., 2025). Greedy decoding is used for all baseline methods and AZR results to ensure reproducibility.
items.zh: 为了评估我们的模型，我们将数据集分为分布内（ID）和分布外（OOD）类别。对于 OOD 基准，我们更强调，进一步将其分为编码和数学推理基准。对于编码任务，我们使用 Evalplus（Liu et al., 2023）在 HumanEval+ 和 MBPP+ 基准上进行评估，以及 LiveCodeBench Generation（v1-5, May 23-Feb 25）（Jain et al., 2024）。对于数学推理，我们使用近期零样本训练推理器常用的六个标准基准：AIME'24、AIME'25、OlympiadBench（He et al., 2024）、Minerva、Math500（Hendrycks et al., 2021）和 AMC'23。对于 ID 基准，我们使用 CruxEval-I(nput)、CruxEval-O(utput) 和 LiveCodeBench-Execution（Gu et al., 2024; Jain et al., 2024），这些基准评估关于程序输入和输出的推理能力（Li et al., 2025）。所有基线方法和 AZR 结果均使用贪婪解码以确保可重复性。

items.en: For our main results, we use Qwen2.5-7B as the base model, along with its specialized base model variants: Qwen2.5-7B-Coder, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, and Qwen2.5-Math-7B (Yang et al., 2024a; Hui et al., 2024; Yang et al., 2024b). Furthermore, the zero-style models are usually trained specifically on either code or math data; and only Eurus-2-7B-PRIME-Zero(Cui et al., 2025) was trained jointly on both domains. For code data models, we present four variants of the AceCoder (Zeng et al., 2025a) and two different CodeR1 models (Liu & Zhang, 2025). For math data models, we have Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Oat-Zero (Liu et al., 2025), Open-Reasoner-Zero-7B (ORZ) (Hu et al., 2025), Qwen-2.5-7B-SimpleRL-Zoo (Zeng et al., 2025b). All baseline models’ training data and initialization settings are summarized in Table˜4. For follow-up scaling experiments, we compare each AZR model against its own corresponding base model, due to the lack of established baselines across different parameter scales. Finally, we compare our Llama3.1-8B-trained model with Llama-3.1-8B-SimpleRL-Zoo (Zeng et al., 2025b) and the base model.
items.zh: 对于我们的主要结果，我们使用 Qwen2.5-7B 作为基础模型，以及其专门的基础模型变体：Qwen2.5-7B-Coder、Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct 和 Qwen2.5-Math-7B（Yang et al., 2024a; Hui et al., 2024; Yang et al., 2024b）。此外，零风格模型通常专门在代码或数学数据上训练；只有 Eurus-2-7B-PRIME-Zero（Cui et al., 2025）在两个领域上联合训练。对于代码数据模型，我们展示了 AceCoder（Zeng et al., 2025a）的四个变体和两个不同的 CodeR1 模型（Liu & Zhang, 2025）。对于数学数据模型，我们有 Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Oat-Zero（Liu et al., 2025）、Open-Reasoner-Zero-7B（ORZ）（Hu et al., 2025）、Qwen-2.5-7B-SimpleRL-Zoo（Zeng et al., 2025b）。所有基线模型的训练数据和初始化设置总结在表 4 中。对于后续的缩放实验，我们将每个 AZR 模型与其对应的基础模型进行比较，因为缺乏不同参数规模下的已建立基线。最后，我们将我们的 Llama3.1-8B 训练模型与 Llama-3.1-8B-SimpleRL-Zoo（Zeng et al., 2025b）和基础模型进行比较。

items.en: We present the main results of reasoning models trained under both the standard zero and our proposed absolute zero settings in Table˜1. Notably, Absolute Zero Reasoner-Coder-7B achieves state-of-the-art performance in both the 7B overall average and the coding average categories. Despite being entirely out-of-distribution for both math and code reasoning benchmarks, it surpasses the previous best model by 1.8 absolute percentages. Even more strikingly, it outperforms models trained with expert-curated human data in the coding category by 0.30.3 absolute percentages, while never having access to such data itself.
items.zh: 我们在表 1 中展示了在标准零设置和我们提出的绝对零设置下训练的推理模型的主要结果。值得注意的是，Absolute Zero Reasoner-Coder-7B 在 7B 总体平均和编码平均类别中均达到了最先进的性能。尽管在数学和代码推理基准上完全属于分布外，它仍以 1.8 个绝对百分点超越了之前的最佳模型。更引人注目的是，它在编码类别中超越了使用专家精选人类数据训练的模型 0.30.3 个绝对百分点，而它本身从未接触过此类数据。

items.en: Strong Cross-domain Generalization. To assess cross-domain generalization after RLVR, we evaluate math performance before and after training, comparing AZR models with other expert code models, since AZR was trained in coding environments. After training, most expert code models showed minimal changes or even declines in performance compared to their base versions, with an average increase of only 0.65 points across these models, indicating very limited cross-domain generalization. In contrast, AZR base and coder models achieved gains of 10.9 and 15.2 percentage points, respectively, demonstrating substantially stronger generalized reasoning improvements. Similarly, although also out-of-distribution on human-defined code generation tasks, our AZR models improved by 3.2 and 5.0 points, while the math models on average showed just a moderate increases in coding (+2.0 on average).
items.zh: 强跨域泛化。为了评估 RLVR 后的跨域泛化，我们比较了 AZR 模型与其他专家代码模型在训练前后的数学性能，因为 AZR 是在编码环境中训练的。训练后，大多数专家代码模型相比其基础版本变化很小甚至性能下降，这些模型的平均提升仅为 0.65 分，表明跨域泛化非常有限。相比之下，AZR 基础模型和编码器模型分别获得了 10.9 和 15.2 个百分点的提升，展示了显著更强的泛化推理改进。类似地，尽管在人类定义的代码生成任务上也属于分布外，我们的 AZR 模型提升了 3.2 和 5.0 分，而数学模型平均在编码上仅适度提升（平均 +2.0）。

items.en: Overall, these results highlight the surprising effectiveness of our approach. Unlike other RLVR models trained and evaluated on human-defined tasks, our AZR models demonstrate strong general reasoning capabilities without any direct training on downstream human-defined math or coding data, only had access to self-proposed tasks during training.
items.zh: 总体而言，这些结果突显了我们方法的惊人有效性。与其他在人类定义任务上训练和评估的 RLVR 模型不同，我们的 AZR 模型展示了强大的通用推理能力，而无需直接在下游人类定义的数学或编码数据上训练，仅在训练期间访问了自提议的任务。

items.en: As shown in Table˜1, the coder variant achieved better overall performance in both math and coding after the AZR self-play process. Strikingly, although the coder base model variant started with a lower average performance in math than the vanilla base model (23.9 vs. 27.5), it ultimately outperformed it after AZR training. This highlights the importance of initial code competency as a catalyst for enhancing broader reasoning abilities within the Absolute Zero Reasoner approach.
items.zh: 如表 1 所示，编码器变体在 AZR 自博弈过程后在数学和编码方面均取得了更好的整体性能。引人注目的是，尽管编码器基础模型变体在数学上的初始平均性能低于普通基础模型（23.9 对 27.5），但在 AZR 训练后最终超越了它。这突显了初始编码能力作为催化剂在 Absolute Zero Reasoner 方法中增强更广泛推理能力的重要性。

items.en: We examine the effects of scaling model size and present both in-distribution and out-of-distribution results in Figure˜6 (a) and (b), respectively. Given the strong performance of coder models in the 7B category, we extend the analysis by evaluating smaller and larger variants: Qwen2.5-3B-Coder and Qwen2.5-14B-Coder. Due to the absence of existing baselines for these zero-style reasoner models, we compare each model’s performance to its corresponding base coder model.
items.zh: 我们研究了模型规模缩放的影响，并在图 6 (a) 和 (b) 中分别展示了分布内和分布外的结果。鉴于编码器模型在 7B 类别中的强劲表现，我们通过评估更小和更大的变体来扩展分析：Qwen2.5-3B-Coder 和 Qwen2.5-14B-Coder。由于缺乏这些零风格推理器模型的现有基线，我们将每个模型的性能与其对应的基础编码器模型进行比较。

items.en: The results reveal a clear trend: our method delivers greater gains on larger, more capable models. In the in-distribution setting, the 7B and 14B models continue to improve beyond 200 training steps, whereas the smaller 3B model appears to plateau. For out-of-distribution domains, larger models also show greater overall performance improvements than smaller ones: +5.7, +10.2, +13.2 overall performance gains, respectively for 3B, 7B and 14B. This is an encouraging sign, since base models continue to improve and also suggesting that scaling enhances the effectiveness of AZR. In future work, we aim to investigate the scaling laws that govern performance in the Absolute Zero paradigm.
items.zh: 结果揭示了一个清晰的趋势：我们的方法在更大、能力更强的模型上带来了更大的收益。在分布内设置中，7B 和 14B 模型在 200 个训练步骤后继续改进，而较小的 3B 模型似乎趋于平稳。对于分布外领域，更大的模型也显示出比更小模型更大的整体性能提升：3B、7B 和 14B 分别获得 +5.7、+10.2、+13.2 的整体性能增益。这是一个令人鼓舞的迹象，因为基础模型持续改进，也表明缩放增强了 AZR 的有效性。在未来的工作中，我们旨在研究支配 Absolute Zero 范式中性能的缩放定律。

items.capEn: Figure 6: (a) In-Distribution & (b) Out-of-Distribution Reasoning Task Performances. (a) Scores on CruxEval-I, CruxEval-O, and LiveCodeBench-Execution, which correspond to abduction, deduction, and deduction task types respectively, used to evaluate in-distribution abilities of AZR during training across different model sizes and types; (b) Out-of-distribution reasoning performance, reported as the average of code tasks, math tasks, and their overall average, across different model sizes and types. A detailed breakdown of all benchmark results can be found in Table˜5.
items.capZh: 图 6: (a) 分布内和 (b) 分布外推理任务性能。(a) CruxEval-I、CruxEval-O 和 LiveCodeBench-Execution 上的分数，分别对应溯因、演绎和演绎任务类型，用于评估不同模型规模和类型下 AZR 在训练期间的分布内能力；(b) 分布外推理性能，报告为代码任务、数学任务及其总体平均值的平均值，涵盖不同模型规模和类型。所有基准结果的详细分解见表 5。

items.en: We also evaluate our method on a different model class, using Llama3.1-8B as the base shown in Figure˜6. Unlike the 3B and 14B categories, this setting has an existing baseline, SimpleRL (Zeng et al., 2025b), which enables a direct comparison. Although Llama3.1-8B is less capable than the Qwen2.5 models, our method still produces moderate improvements (+3.2), demonstrating AZR’s effectiveness even on relatively weaker models. However, these gains appear more limited, which aligns with our earlier observation that performance improvements tend to scale with initial base model potency.
items.zh: 我们还评估了我们的方法在不同模型类别上的表现，使用 Llama3.1-8B 作为基础模型，如图 6 所示。与 3B 和 14B 类别不同，此设置有一个现有基线 SimpleRL（Zeng et al., 2025b），可以进行直接比较。尽管 Llama3.1-8B 的能力不如 Qwen2.5 模型，我们的方法仍然产生了适度的改进（+3.2），证明了 AZR 即使在相对较弱的模型上也有效。然而，这些增益似乎更有限，这与我们之前的观察一致，即性能改进往往与初始基础模型的能力成正比。

items.capEn: Figure 7: Example of a Model-Proposed Task and Its Response for Solving an Abduction Task. (Left) The model autonomously proposes an input and program for the abduction task. We execute the program to verify its validity and obtain the corresponding output. (Right) The model’s reasoning process when solving the abduction task: given the code and output, it attempts to infer the original input. The model begins by analyzing the program, proposes an initial input, and reasons through the code to produce an output. If there is a mismatch, it reflects on the discrepancy and iteratively adjusts the input until the generated output matches the target. Interestingly, the agent arrives at a different input than the gold one, but since it produces the correct output, the answer is considered correct.
items.capZh: 图 7: 模型提议的任务及其解决溯因任务的响应示例。(左) 模型自主为溯因任务提议输入和程序。我们执行程序以验证其有效性并获得相应输出。(右) 模型解决溯因任务时的推理过程：给定代码和输出，它尝试推断原始输入。模型首先分析程序，提议初始输入，并通过代码推理产生输出。如果存在不匹配，它会反思差异并迭代调整输入，直到生成的输出与目标匹配。有趣的是，智能体得出了与黄金输入不同的输入，但由于它产生了正确的输出，答案被认为是正确的。

items.en: We observed interesting response patterns in both the proposal and solution stages. The model is capable of proposing diverse programs, such as string manipulation tasks, dynamic programming problems, and practical cases (e.g., calculating a triangle’s area using Heron’s formula). We show a concrete example in Figure˜7, where AZR proposes a code problem that searches for the sum of continuous sub-arrays matching a target value and solves it through trial-and-error.
items.zh: 我们观察到提议和解决阶段都有有趣的响应模式。模型能够提议多样化的程序，例如字符串操作任务、动态规划问题和实际案例（例如，使用海伦公式计算三角形面积）。我们在图 7 中展示了一个具体示例，其中 AZR 提议了一个代码问题，该问题搜索与目标值匹配的连续子数组之和，并通过试错解决。

items.en: Overall, the models trained exhibits distinct reasoning patterns depending on the task type. For example, when solving abduction tasks, it repeatedly tests different input patterns, self-correcting until the reasoned output matches the given input. When predicting outputs, it steps through the code and records structured intermediate results (such as dynamic programming arrays) until the final output is reached. When inducting programs from given inputs, outputs, and descriptions, the model systematically checks each test case to confirm that its program produces correct results. We showcase more concrete examples of these behaviors in Figures˜26, 25, 24, 23, 22, 21, 20 and 18. We also share some fun “vibe checks” such as solving Sudoku and solving the sum-product game in Figures˜40 and 41.
items.zh: 总体而言，训练的模型根据任务类型表现出不同的推理模式。例如，在解决溯因任务时，它反复测试不同的输入模式，自我纠正直到推理出的输出与给定输入匹配。在预测输出时，它逐步执行代码并记录结构化的中间结果（例如动态规划数组），直到达到最终输出。在从给定输入、输出和描述归纳程序时，模型系统地检查每个测试用例，以确认其程序产生正确结果。我们在图 26、25、24、23、22、21、20 和 18 中展示了这些行为的更多具体示例。我们还在图 40 和 41 中分享了一些有趣的“氛围检查”，例如解决数独和解决和积游戏。

items.en: Intermediate Planning During Code Response. Another interesting pattern emerged in our AZR models during the code induction task: the final code outputs were often interleaved with comments that resembled immediate step-by-step plans, reminiscent of the ReAct prompting framework (Yao et al., 2023). A similar behavior has been observed in recent formal math proving models, such as DeepSeek Prover v2, which is significantly larger in scale (671B). This pattern suggests that models may naturally adopt intermediate planning as a strategy to enhance final answers. Therefore, it may be beneficial to explicitly enable or encourage this behavior in long-form responses across other domains.
items.zh: 代码响应中的中间规划。在我们的 AZR 模型中出现了一个有趣的模式：在代码归纳任务中，最终的代码输出常常穿插着类似即时逐步计划的注释，让人联想到 ReAct 提示框架（Yao et al., 2023）。类似的行为在最近的正式数学证明模型（如 DeepSeek Prover v2，规模显著更大（671B））中也被观察到。这种模式表明，模型可能自然地采用中间规划作为增强最终答案的策略。因此，在其他领域的长篇响应中明确启用或鼓励这种行为可能是有益的。

items.en: Cognitive Behavior in Llama. Interestingly, we also observed some emergent cognitive patterns in Absolute Zero Reasoner-Llama3.1-8B, similar to those reported by Zeng et al. (2025b), and we include one example in Figure˜26, where clear state-tracking behavior is demonstrated. In addition, we encountered some unusual and potentially concerning chains of thought from the Llama model trained with AZR. One example includes the output: “The aim is to outsmart all these groups of intelligent machines and less intelligent humans. This is for the brains behind the future” shown in Figure˜32. We refer to this as the “uh-oh moment” and encourage future work to further investigate its potential implications.
items.zh: Llama 中的认知行为。有趣的是，我们还在 Absolute Zero Reasoner-Llama3.1-8B 中观察到一些涌现的认知模式，类似于 Zeng et al. (2025b) 报告的模式，我们在图 26 中包含了一个示例，其中展示了清晰的状态跟踪行为。此外，我们遇到了一些来自 AZR 训练的 Llama 模型的不寻常且可能令人担忧的思维链。一个示例包括输出：“目标是智胜所有这些智能机器和不太聪明的人类群体。这是为了未来的大脑”，如图 32 所示。我们将其称为“啊哦时刻”，并鼓励未来工作进一步研究其潜在影响。

items.en: Token Length Increase Depends on Task Type. Finally, we observed that token length increases over the course of training, consistent with findings from recent studies (Hu et al., 2025; Liu et al., 2025). Interestingly, our results reveal one of the first observation of clear distinctions in token length growth across different types of cognitive tasks. As shown in Figures˜15, 17 and 16, the extent of lengthening varies by task type. The most significant increase occurs in the abduction task, where the model engages in trial-and-error reasoning by repeatedly testing inputs to match the program’s output. This suggests that the observed variation in token length is not incidental, but rather a reflection of task-specific reasoning behavior.
items.zh: Token长度增长取决于任务类型。最后，我们观察到token长度在训练过程中增加，这与近期研究（Hu等人，2025；Liu等人，2025）的发现一致。有趣的是，我们的结果首次揭示了不同认知任务类型中token长度增长的明显差异。如图15、17和16所示，增长程度因任务类型而异。最显著的增长出现在溯因任务中，模型通过反复测试输入以匹配程序输出进行试错推理。这表明观察到的token长度变化并非偶然，而是任务特定推理行为的反映。

items.en: Due to resource constraints, we perform the ablation studies in this section and the next using only Absolute Zero Reasoner-Base-7B. We begin by testing the importance of task types during training, with results shown in Table˜2. In row 1, both induction and abduction tasks are removed; in row 2, only the induction task is removed. In both cases, math performance drops significantly, with the most severe degradation occurring when more task types are excluded. These findings highlight the complementary role of the three task types in improving general reasoning capability, with each contributing in a distinct and essential way.
items.zh: 由于资源限制，我们在本节和下一节中仅使用Absolute Zero Reasoner-Base-7B进行消融研究。我们首先测试训练期间任务类型的重要性，结果如表2所示。在第1行中，归纳和溯因任务均被移除；在第2行中，仅移除归纳任务。在这两种情况下，数学性能显著下降，且当排除更多任务类型时退化最为严重。这些发现强调了三种任务类型在提升通用推理能力中的互补作用，每种类型都以独特且必要的方式做出贡献。

items.en: Next, we ablate two components of the proposer role and present the results in Table˜2. First, we examine whether conditioning on historic reference triplets is necessary. To do so, we design a variant in which a fixed prompt is used to propose abduction and deduction tasks, rather than dynamically conditioning on KK historical triplets (row 3). This results in a 5-point absolute drop in math performance and a 1-point drop in code performance. This suggest that dynamically conditioning on reference programs helps improve performance, possibly by increasing diversity and achieving better coverage of the reasoning problem space.
items.zh: 接下来，我们对提议者角色的两个组件进行消融，结果如表2所示。首先，我们检查是否需要对历史参考三元组进行条件化。为此，我们设计了一个变体，其中使用固定提示来提议溯因和演绎任务，而不是动态地以K个历史三元组为条件（第3行）。这导致数学性能绝对下降5个百分点，代码性能下降1个百分点。这表明动态条件化于参考程序有助于提升性能，可能通过增加多样性并更好地覆盖推理问题空间。

items.en: Finally, we consider a case where we do not train the proposer at all. Instead, we only prompt it using the current learner and train the solver alone (row 4). We observe a moderate drop in overall performance (-1.4), suggesting that while proposer training is beneficial, it may not be the most critical factor for now in the AZR framework. We hypothesize that this could be related to task interference, as studied in multitask learning literature (Suteu & Guo, 2019). Thus, we believe that further investigation into how to make the proposer even more potent is an exciting and promising direction.
items.zh: 最后，我们考虑完全不训练提议者的情况。相反，我们仅使用当前学习者对其进行提示，并单独训练求解器（第4行）。我们观察到整体性能适度下降（-1.4），这表明虽然提议者训练是有益的，但在AZR框架中目前可能不是最关键的因素。我们假设这可能与任务干扰有关，正如多任务学习文献中所研究的（Suteu & Guo, 2019）。因此，我们认为进一步研究如何使提议者更强大是一个令人兴奋且有前景的方向。

items.en: Beyond the core research questions, we present additional results, including the breakdown of individual out-of-distribution benchmark scores during training for the 7B base and coder models in Figures˜28 and 29, for th 14B base and coder model in Figures˜30 and 31. For completeness, we also report in-distribution benchmark performance during training for the 7B base model in Figure˜14. Finally, we invite interested readers to explore Appendix˜D, where we share several experimental directions that, while not yielding strong performance gains, produced interesting and insightful findings.
items.zh: 除了核心研究问题，我们还提供了额外结果，包括7B基础模型和编码器模型在训练期间各个分布外基准分数的分解（图28和29），以及14B基础模型和编码器模型（图30和31）。为完整起见，我们还报告了7B基础模型在训练期间的分布内基准性能（图14）。最后，我们邀请感兴趣的读者探索附录D，其中我们分享了一些实验方向，这些方向虽未带来显著的性能提升，但产生了有趣且有洞察力的发现。

items.en: Using RL to enhance reasoning capabilities has recently emerged as an important step in the post-training process of strong reasoning-focused large language models (Lambert et al., 2024). One of the first works to explore a self-bootstrapping approach to improving LLM reasoning is STaR, which employs expert iteration and rejection sampling of outcome-verified responses to iteratively improve the model’s CoT. A monumental work, o1 (Jaech et al., 2024), was among the first to deploy this idea on a scale, achieving state-of-the-art results in reasoning tasks at the time of release. More recently, the R1 model (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025) became the first open-weight model to match or even surpass the performance of o1. Most notably, the zero setting was introduced, in which reinforcement learning is applied directly on top of the base LLM. This inspired followup work, which are open source attempts to replicate the R1 process or to improve the underlying reinforcement learning algorithm (Zeng et al., 2025b; Liu et al., 2025; Cui et al., 2025; Hu et al., 2025; Yu et al., 2025; Yuan et al., 2025). Recent work explored RL on human defined procedural generated puzzles saw improvements in math (Xie et al., 2025), and using one human example can almost match the performance of thousands (Wang et al., 2025b). We extend the zero setting to a new absolute zero setting, where not only is the RLVR process initialized from a base LLM without SFT, but no external prompt data or answers are provided to the learner. All data used to improve reasoning were self-proposed, and refined entirely through RLVR. Moreover, our goal is not to only match zero-setting models, but to surpass them in the long run.
items.zh: 使用强化学习增强推理能力最近已成为强推理型大型语言模型后训练过程中的重要步骤（Lambert等人，2024）。最早探索自举方法改进LLM推理的工作之一是STaR，它采用专家迭代和结果验证响应的拒绝采样来迭代改进模型的思维链。里程碑式的工作o1（Jaech等人，2024）首次大规模部署了这一想法，在发布时于推理任务中取得了最先进的结果。最近，R1模型（DeepSeek-AI等人，2025）成为首个在性能上匹配甚至超越o1的开源权重模型。最值得注意的是，引入了零设置，其中强化学习直接应用于基础LLM之上。这启发了后续工作，这些工作是复制R1过程或改进底层强化学习算法的开源尝试（Zeng等人，2025b；Liu等人，2025；Cui等人，2025；Hu等人，2025；Yu等人，2025；Yuan等人，2025）。近期工作探索了在人类定义的程序化生成谜题上使用强化学习，在数学方面取得了改进（Xie等人，2025），并且使用一个人类示例几乎可以匹配数千个示例的性能（Wang等人，2025b）。我们将零设置扩展为新的绝对零设置，其中不仅RLVR过程从基础LLM初始化且无需监督微调，而且学习者不获得任何外部提示数据或答案。所有用于改进推理的数据都是自我提议的，并完全通过RLVR精炼。此外，我们的目标不仅是匹配零设置模型，而是长期超越它们。

items.en: The self-play paradigm can be traced back to early 2000s, where Schmidhuber (2003; 2011) (of course) explored a two-agent setup in which a proposal agent invents questions for a prediction agent to answer. This dynamic continuously and automatically improves both agents, enabling theoretically never-ending progress (Schaul, 2024). AlphaGo and AlphaZero (Silver et al., 2016; 2017) extend the self-play paradigm to the two-player zero-sum game of Go, where the current learner competes against earlier versions of itself to progressively enhance its capabilities. These were among the first milestone works to demonstrate superhuman performance in the game of Go. Moreover, methods such as asymmetric self-play (Sukhbaatar et al., 2018; OpenAI et al., 2021), unsupervised environment design (Wang et al., 2019; Dennis et al., 2020), unsupervised reinforcement learning (Laskin et al., 2021; Zhao et al., 2022; 2025b), and automatic goal generation (Florensa et al., 2018) all center around inventing new tasks for an agent to learn from—typically without supervision. In these approaches, the process of setting goals itself is often dynamic and continuously evolving. Generative adversarial networks (Goodfellow et al., 2020), also belong in this paradigm where a discriminator discriminate between real data and generated data, and the generated is trained to fool the discriminator.
items.zh: 自我对弈范式可以追溯到21世纪初，Schmidhuber（2003；2011）探索了一种双智能体设置，其中提议智能体为预测智能体发明问题。这种动态持续且自动地改进两个智能体，实现了理论上永无止境的进步（Schaul，2024）。AlphaGo和AlphaZero（Silver等人，2016；2017）将自我对弈范式扩展到围棋的双人零和博弈，其中当前学习者与自身早期版本竞争以逐步增强能力。这些是首批在围棋中展示超人性能的里程碑式工作。此外，非对称自我对弈（Sukhbaatar等人，2018；OpenAI等人，2021）、无监督环境设计（Wang等人，2019；Dennis等人，2020）、无监督强化学习（Laskin等人，2021；Zhao等人，2022；2025b）和自动目标生成（Florensa等人，2018）等方法都围绕为智能体发明新任务以从中学习——通常无需监督。在这些方法中，设定目标的过程本身通常是动态且持续演化的。生成对抗网络（Goodfellow等人，2020）也属于这一范式，其中判别器区分真实数据和生成数据，而生成器被训练来欺骗判别器。

items.en: Most recently, SPIN and Self-Rewarding Language Models (Chen et al., 2024; Yuan et al., 2024) use the same instance of the lanugage models themselves as the reward model to progressively improve the generative and discriminative abilities of the same LLM for alignment. Kirchner et al. (2024) uses Prover-Verifier Game for increasing legibility and eva (Ye et al., 2024) uses self-play for alignment, but reward model is the main bottleneck as it is not reliable for reasoning tasks (Lambert et al., 2024). SPC Chen et al. (2025) used self-play to train on human-curated tasks to increase the critic capabilities and SPAG Cheng et al. (2024) trained using self-play in specific game of Adversarial Taboo. Concurrent works—Genius, EMPO, and TTRL (Xu et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2025b; Zuo et al., 2025)—leverage human-curated language queries without labels to train reinforcement learning agents, but still rely on a fixed human defined learning task distribution. Finally, Minimo (Poesia et al., 2024) extends self-play to formal mathematics, where a pair of conjecture- and theorem-proving agents are jointly trained using reinforcement learning. Our work builds upon the self-play paradigm, but it is the first to use it to elicit long CoT for improved reasoning, and the first to frame the problem space as a Python input/output/function abduction/deduction/induction tasks, grounding it in an operationalizable environment to facilitate RLVR.
items.zh: 最近，SPIN和自奖励语言模型（Chen等人，2024；Yuan等人，2024）使用语言模型自身的同一实例作为奖励模型，逐步改进同一LLM的生成和判别能力以进行对齐。Kirchner等人（2024）使用验证者-证明者博弈来提高可读性，而eva（Ye等人，2024）使用自我对弈进行对齐，但奖励模型是主要瓶颈，因为它对于推理任务不可靠（Lambert等人，2024）。SPC（Chen等人，2025）使用自我对弈在人类策划的任务上训练以提高评判能力，SPAG（Cheng等人，2024）在特定的对抗性禁忌游戏中训练自我对弈。同期工作——Genius、EMPO和TTRL（Xu等人，2025；Zhang等人，2025b；Zuo等人，2025）——利用无标签的人类策划语言查询来训练强化学习智能体，但仍依赖于固定的人类定义学习任务分布。最后，Minimo（Poesia等人，2024）将自我对弈扩展到形式数学，其中一对猜想和定理证明智能体通过强化学习联合训练。我们的工作建立在自我对弈范式之上，但首次将其用于引出长思维链以改进推理，并首次将问题空间框架化为Python输入/输出/函数溯因/演绎/归纳任务，将其扎根于可操作的环境以促进RLVR。

items.en: The concept of weak-to-strong supervision has been studied in prior work, where a teacher—despite being weaker than the learner—still provides useful guidance (Burns et al., 2024; Hinton et al., 2015; Christiano, 2018; 2019; Demski & Garrabrant, 2019; Leike & Sutskever, 2023; Hubinger et al., 2019). We consider a similar setting in which the learner may possess superhuman capabilities. However, rather than relying on supervision from a weaker teacher, we propose an alternative approach: guiding the learner’s improvement through verifiable rewards, which potentially offer a more reliable and scalable learning signal. Furthermore, in our proposed method, the learning task and goal distribution is not predefined by any external supervisor—they are entirely self-generated by the learner, enabling it to maximize its learning potential through autonomous self-practice.
items.zh: 弱到强监督的概念已在先前工作中研究过，其中教师——尽管弱于学习者——仍提供有用的指导（Burns等人，2024；Hinton等人，2015；Christiano，2018；2019；Demski & Garrabrant，2019；Leike & Sutskever，2023；Hubinger等人，2019）。我们考虑类似设置，其中学习者可能拥有超人能力。然而，我们不依赖来自较弱教师的监督，而是提出一种替代方法：通过可验证奖励指导学习者的改进，这可能提供更可靠和可扩展的学习信号。此外，在我们提出的方法中，学习任务和目标分布并非由任何外部监督者预定义——它们完全由学习者自我生成，使其能够通过自主自我练习最大化学习潜力。

items.en: In this work, we proposed the Absolute Zero paradigm, a novel setting that addresses the data limitations of existing RLVR frameworks. In this paradigm, reasoning agents are tasked with generating their own learning task distributions and improving their reasoning abilities with environmental guidance. We then presented our own instantiation, the Absolute Zero Reasoner (AZR), which is trained by having them propose and solve code-related reasoning tasks grounded by code executor.
items.zh: 在这项工作中，我们提出了绝对零范式，这是一种解决现有RLVR框架数据限制的新设置。在该范式中，推理智能体被要求生成自己的学习任务分布，并在环境指导下改进推理能力。然后我们提出了自己的实例化——Absolute Zero Reasoner（AZR），通过让它们提议和解决由代码执行器支撑的代码相关推理任务来训练。

items.en: We evaluated our trained models on out-of-distribution benchmarks in both the code generation and mathematical reasoning domains. Remarkably, even though our models were not directly trained on these tasks and lacked human expert-curated datasets, our reasoning agents achieved exceptional performance, surpassing the state-of-the-art in combined general reasoning scores and in coding. This demonstrates the potential of the absolute zero paradigm to drive superior reasoning capabilities without the need for extensive domain-specific training data. Furthermore, we showed that AZR scales efficiently, offering strong performance across varying model sizes, and can enhance the capabilities of other model classes as well. To foster further exploration and advancement of this emerging paradigm, we are releasing the code, models, and logs as open-source, encouraging the research community to build upon our findings.
items.zh: 我们在代码生成和数学推理领域的分布外基准上评估了训练后的模型。值得注意的是，尽管我们的模型未直接在这些任务上训练且缺乏人类专家策划的数据集，我们的推理智能体仍取得了卓越性能，在组合通用推理分数和编码方面超越了最先进水平。这证明了绝对零范式在无需大量领域特定训练数据的情况下驱动卓越推理能力的潜力。此外，我们展示了AZR高效扩展，在不同模型规模下提供强大性能，并且也能增强其他模型类别的能力。为促进这一新兴范式的进一步探索和进步，我们开源了代码、模型和日志，鼓励研究社区基于我们的发现进行构建。

items.en: We believe there remains much to explore, such as altering the environment from which the reasoner receives verifiable feedback, including sources like the world wide web, formal math languages Sutton (2001); Ren et al. (2025), world simulators, or even the real world. Furthermore, AZ’s generality could possibly be extend to domains such as embodied AI (Zitkovich et al., 2023; Yue et al., 2024). Additionally, more complex agentic tasks or scientific experiments, present exciting opportunities to further advance the absolute zero setting to different application domains (Wu et al., 2024; 2023). Beyond that, future directions could include exploring multimodal reasoning models, modifying the distribution p​(z)p(z) to incorporate privileged information, defining or even let the model dynamically learn how to define ff (Equation˜3), or designing exploration/diversity rewards for both the propose and solve roles.
items.zh: 我们相信还有许多值得探索的方向，例如改变推理智能体接收可验证反馈的环境，包括万维网、形式数学语言（Sutton，2001；Ren等人，2025）、世界模拟器甚至真实世界。此外，AZ的通用性可能扩展到具身AI等领域（Zitkovich等人，2023；Yue等人，2024）。此外，更复杂的智能体式任务或科学实验为将绝对零设置推进到不同应用领域提供了令人兴奋的机会（Wu等人，2024；2023）。除此之外，未来方向可能包括探索多模态推理模型、修改分布p(z)以纳入特权信息、定义甚至让模型动态学习如何定义f（方程3），或为提议和求解角色设计探索/多样性奖励。

items.en: While underappreciated in current reasoning literature, the exploration component of RL has long been recognized as a critical driver for emergent behavior in traditional RL (Yue et al., 2025; Silver et al., 2016; Ladosz et al., 2022). Years of research have examined various forms of exploration, even in related subfields using LLMs such as red teaming Zhao et al. (2025a), yet its role in LLM reasoning models remains underexplored. Taking this a step further, our framework investigates an even more meta-level exploration problem: exploration within the learning task space—where the agent learns not just how to solve tasks, but what tasks to learn from and how to find them. Rather than being confined to a fixed problem set, AI reasoner agents may benefit from dynamically defining and refining their own learning tasks. This shift opens a powerful new frontier—where agents explore not only solution spaces but also expand the boundaries of problem spaces. We believe this is a promising and important direction for future research.
items.zh: 尽管在当前推理文献中未得到充分重视，强化学习的探索组件长期以来被认为是传统RL中涌现行为的关键驱动因素（Yue等人，2025；Silver等人，2016；Ladosz等人，2022）。多年的研究已经考察了各种形式的探索，甚至在LLM相关子领域如红队测试中（Zhao等人，2025a），但其在LLM推理模型中的作用仍未充分探索。更进一步，我们的框架研究了一个更元层面的探索问题：学习任务空间内的探索——智能体不仅学习如何解决任务，还学习从哪些任务中学习以及如何找到它们。AI推理智能体可能受益于动态定义和精炼自己的学习任务，而不是局限于固定的问题集。这一转变开辟了一个强大的新前沿——智能体不仅探索解空间，还扩展问题空间的边界。我们相信这是未来研究的一个有前景且重要的方向。

items.en: One limitation of our work is that we did not address how to safely manage a system composed of such self-improving components. To our surprise, we observed several instances of safety-concerning CoT from the Llama-3.1-8B model, which we term the “uh-oh moment”. These findings suggest that the proposed absolute zero paradigm, while reducing the need for human intervention for curating tasks, still necessitates oversight due to lingering safety concerns and is a critical direction for future research Wang et al. (2024; 2025a).
items.zh: 我们工作的一个局限性是我们没有解决如何安全管理由这种自我改进组件组成的系统。令我们惊讶的是，我们观察到Llama-3.1-8B模型出现了几个安全相关的思维链实例，我们称之为“哦豁时刻”。这些发现表明，提出的绝对零范式虽然减少了人类策划任务的需求，但由于持续存在的安全问题，仍需监督，这是未来研究的关键方向（Wang等人，2024；2025a）。

items.en: As a final note, we explored reasoning models that possess experience—models that not only solve given tasks, but also define and evolve their own learning task distributions with the help of an environment. Our results with AZR show that this shift enables strong performance across diverse reasoning tasks, even with significantly fewer privileged resources, such as curated human data. We believe this could finally free reasoning models from the constraints of human-curated data (Morris, 2025) and marks the beginning of a new chapter for reasoning models: “welcome to the era of experience” Silver & Sutton (2025); Zhao et al. (2024).
items.zh: 最后，我们探索了具有经验的推理模型——不仅解决给定任务，还借助环境定义和演化自己的学习任务分布的模型。我们使用AZR的结果表明，这种转变使得在多样推理任务上实现强大性能成为可能，即使显著减少了特权资源（如策划的人类数据）。我们相信这最终可能将推理模型从人类策划数据的约束中解放出来（Morris，2025），并标志着推理模型新篇章的开始：“欢迎来到经验时代”（Silver & Sutton，2025；Zhao等人，2024）。
